title: Truck Driver Wage Search Surge
thesis: Spikes in truck driver job and wage searches signal labor market tightening in freight, often preceding freight rate increases and logistics sector rallies. Tight trucking labor markets correlate with stronger freight demand and pricing power for logistics and industrial companies.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'truck driver jobs' and 'truck driver salary'
signal_logic: If weekly Google Trends index for 'truck driver jobs' or 'truck driver salary' exceeds its 12-week median by 40% or more
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly Google Trends index for 'truck driver jobs' or 'truck driver salary' exceeds its 12-week median by 40% or more Exit: Close after 3 weeks or when index falls below 20% above median
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for 'truck driver jobs' and 'truck driver salary' via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Labor shortages in transportation are chronic; wage/job search spikes occur 4–6 times per year as seasonal demand fluctuates.

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'truck driver jobs' and 'truck driver salary'

schema.behavior: Spikes in truck driver job and wage searches signal labor market tightening in freight, often preceding freight rate increases and logistics sector rallies.

market_impact.rationale: Tight trucking labor markets correlate with stronger freight demand and pricing power for logistics and industrial companies.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly Google Trends index for 'truck driver jobs' or 'truck driver salary' exceeds its 12-week median by 40% or more
trade_logic.exit: Close after 3 weeks or when index falls below 20% above median

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Labor shortages in transportation are chronic; wage/job search spikes occur 4–6 times per year as seasonal demand fluctuates.

title: Manufacturing Input Cost Pressure Fred Acceleration
thesis: Week-over-week acceleration in PPI for inputs signals rising cost pressures, which trigger equity rotation out of materials and into defensive sectors. Materials companies face margin compression when input costs accelerate faster than they can pass through prices.
data_sources: FRED series PPI for intermediate materials (PPIICMM) weekly aggregation
signal_logic: If 2-week change in PPI intermediate materials exceeds the 26-week rolling average change by 0.3% or more
entry_exit: Entry: If 2-week change in PPI intermediate materials exceeds the 26-week rolling average change by 0.3% or more Exit: Exit after 2 weeks or when acceleration reverses below median
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series PPI for intermediate materials (PPIICMM) weekly aggregation via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: FRED PPI data releases weekly; commodity and energy volatility produce input cost swings frequently enough to trigger monthly.

schema.idea: Manufacturing Input Cost Pressure FRED Acceleration

data.source: FRED series PPI for intermediate materials (PPIICMM) weekly aggregation

schema.behavior: Week-over-week acceleration in PPI for inputs signals rising cost pressures, which trigger equity rotation out of materials and into defensive sectors.

market_impact.rationale: Materials companies face margin compression when input costs accelerate faster than they can pass through prices.

trade_logic.entry: If 2-week change in PPI intermediate materials exceeds the 26-week rolling average change by 0.3% or more
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 2 weeks or when acceleration reverses below median

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: FRED PPI data releases weekly; commodity and energy volatility produce input cost swings frequently enough to trigger monthly.

title: Commodity Shortage News Volume Burst
thesis: Daily spike in supply chain and commodity shortage news mentions signals market anxiety about logistics constraints and inflation. Shortage narratives boost commodity and materials equity valuations as investors price in scarcity premiums.
data_sources: RSS/news feed count for 'supply chain bottleneck' and 'commodity shortage' articles via rss_count adapter
signal_logic: If daily news article count for 'supply chain' OR 'commodity shortage' exceeds 90-day median by 60% or more
entry_exit: Entry: If daily news article count for 'supply chain' OR 'commodity shortage' exceeds 90-day median by 60% or more Exit: Exit after 5 trading days or when article count falls to median
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS/news feed count for 'supply chain bottleneck' and 'commodity shortage' articles via rss_count adapter via scrape (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Geopolitical events, weather, and logistics disruptions reliably produce supply chain news spikes weekly.

data.source: RSS/news feed count for 'supply chain bottleneck' and 'commodity shortage' articles via rss_count adapter

schema.behavior: Daily spike in supply chain and commodity shortage news mentions signals market anxiety about logistics constraints and inflation.

market_impact.rationale: Shortage narratives boost commodity and materials equity valuations as investors price in scarcity premiums.

trade_logic.entry: If daily news article count for 'supply chain' OR 'commodity shortage' exceeds 90-day median by 60% or more
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 5 trading days or when article count falls to median

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Geopolitical events, weather, and logistics disruptions reliably produce supply chain news spikes weekly.

title: Ev Charger Buildout Acceleration Play
thesis: Rapid week-over-week growth in public EV charger deployment signals infrastructure capex cycles and local clean energy investment momentum. EV charging buildout drives industrial capex, construction jobs, and equipment orders for utilities and industrial suppliers.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap EV charger count by state/region weekly snapshot
signal_logic: If weekly EV charger additions exceed 52-week median by 50% or more in any 3-state cluster
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly EV charger additions exceed 52-week median by 50% or more in any 3-state cluster Exit: After 4 weeks or if weekly additions fall back to median
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap EV charger count by state/region weekly snapshot via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Regional infrastructure grants and utility capex cycles create recurring deployment bursts; statistically fires 2–3 times per quarter.

data.source: OpenChargeMap EV charger count by state/region weekly snapshot

schema.behavior: Rapid week-over-week growth in public EV charger deployment signals infrastructure capex cycles and local clean energy investment momentum.

market_impact.rationale: EV charging buildout drives industrial capex, construction jobs, and equipment orders for utilities and industrial suppliers.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly EV charger additions exceed 52-week median by 50% or more in any 3-state cluster
trade_logic.exit: After 4 weeks or if weekly additions fall back to median

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Regional infrastructure grants and utility capex cycles create recurring deployment bursts; statistically fires 2–3 times per quarter.

title: Weather Extreme Disruption Energy Demand Spike
thesis: Days with extreme heat/cold >2 std dev from normal drive electricity demand spikes and boost utility/energy pricing. Extreme weather drives peak electricity demand and margin expansion for utilities and power generators.
data_sources: Open-Meteo daily temperature anomaly by region + EIA electricity demand
signal_logic: If daily temperature anomaly >2 std dev from 20-year normal for region AND EIA electricity demand spike >8% from prior day
entry_exit: Entry: If daily temperature anomaly >2 std dev from 20-year normal for region AND EIA electricity demand spike >8% from prior day Exit: Exit after 3 trading days or when temperature normalizes
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Open-Meteo daily temperature anomaly by region + EIA electricity demand via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal temperature extremes produce 3–6 multi-day heat/cold waves annually; each fires signal reliably.

schema.idea: Weather Extreme Disruption Energy Demand Spike

data.source: Open-Meteo daily temperature anomaly by region + EIA electricity demand

schema.behavior: Days with extreme heat/cold >2 std dev from normal drive electricity demand spikes and boost utility/energy pricing.

market_impact.rationale: Extreme weather drives peak electricity demand and margin expansion for utilities and power generators.

trade_logic.entry: If daily temperature anomaly >2 std dev from 20-year normal for region AND EIA electricity demand spike >8% from prior day
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 3 trading days or when temperature normalizes

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal temperature extremes produce 3–6 multi-day heat/cold waves annually; each fires signal reliably.

title: Stock Buyback Momentum Reversion Signal
thesis: Multi-day surge in relative trading volume on mega-cap tech/growth stocks often coincides with corporate buyback windows; pullback signals mean reversion. Buyback-driven rallies are mechanical; when volume normalizes, momentum fades and reverses.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily volume and price for XLK, XLY, XLC relative to SPY
signal_logic: If XLK or XLC volume exceeds 30-day median by 40% for 2 consecutive days AND price up >1%, short on day 3
entry_exit: Entry: If XLK or XLC volume exceeds 30-day median by 40% for 2 consecutive days AND price up >1%, short on day 3 Exit: Exit after 5 trading days or if price rallies another 2%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily volume and price for XLK, XLY, XLC relative to SPY via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Corporate buyback windows occur quarterly; volume spikes happen 2–3 times per month during window periods.

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily volume and price for XLK, XLY, XLC relative to SPY

schema.behavior: Multi-day surge in relative trading volume on mega-cap tech/growth stocks often coincides with corporate buyback windows; pullback signals mean reversion.

market_impact.rationale: Buyback-driven rallies are mechanical; when volume normalizes, momentum fades and reverses.

trade_logic.entry: If XLK or XLC volume exceeds 30-day median by 40% for 2 consecutive days AND price up >1%, short on day 3
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 5 trading days or if price rallies another 2%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Corporate buyback windows occur quarterly; volume spikes happen 2–3 times per month during window periods.

title: Unemployment Claims Spike Jobless Fear Burst
thesis: Week-over-week jump in jobless claims exceeding seasonal adjustment by 15% or more signals labor market stress and consumer anxiety. Jobless claims spikes correlate with consumer pullback on discretionary spending and equity de-risking.
data_sources: FRED initial jobless claims (ICSA) daily/weekly release
signal_logic: If weekly jobless claims spike >15% above prior week and exceed 52-week median by 20%
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly jobless claims spike >15% above prior week and exceed 52-week median by 20% Exit: Exit after 2 weeks or when claims revert to median
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED initial jobless claims (ICSA) daily/weekly release via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Jobless claims volatility is weekly and frequent; threshold fires 6–10 times per year during normal volatility.

schema.idea: Unemployment Claims Spike Jobless Fear Burst

data.source: FRED initial jobless claims (ICSA) daily/weekly release

schema.behavior: Week-over-week jump in jobless claims exceeding seasonal adjustment by 15% or more signals labor market stress and consumer anxiety.

market_impact.rationale: Jobless claims spikes correlate with consumer pullback on discretionary spending and equity de-risking.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly jobless claims spike >15% above prior week and exceed 52-week median by 20%
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 2 weeks or when claims revert to median

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Jobless claims volatility is weekly and frequent; threshold fires 6–10 times per year during normal volatility.

title: Airline Load Factor Collapse Fear Trade
thesis: Sharp 2+ week drop in airline load factors signals demand weakness and signals bearish travel/discretionary sentiment. Airline load factor collapse is a leading indicator of consumer travel pullback and discretionary spending weakness.
data_sources: BTS airline load factor and capacity data weekly aggregation
signal_logic: If weekly airline load factor drops 2+ points below its 8-week moving average
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly airline load factor drops 2+ points below its 8-week moving average Exit: Exit after 3 weeks or when load factor rebounds above 8-week MA
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use BTS airline load factor and capacity data weekly aggregation via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal travel demand swings and economic uncertainty produce 3–4 load factor dips per year of 2+ points.

schema.idea: Airline Load Factor Collapse Fear Trade

data.source: BTS airline load factor and capacity data weekly aggregation

schema.behavior: Sharp 2+ week drop in airline load factors signals demand weakness and signals bearish travel/discretionary sentiment.

market_impact.rationale: Airline load factor collapse is a leading indicator of consumer travel pullback and discretionary spending weakness.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly airline load factor drops 2+ points below its 8-week moving average
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 3 weeks or when load factor rebounds above 8-week MA

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal travel demand swings and economic uncertainty produce 3–4 load factor dips per year of 2+ points.

title: Earthquake Swarm Construction Material Demand Surge
thesis: Multi-day earthquake swarms (>5 events in 48 hrs, magnitude 3.5+) in urban areas signal impending rebuilding demand and construction/materials activity. Post-earthquake reconstruction drives cement, steel, and materials demand.
data_sources: USGS earthquake activity API for daily counts and magnitude by region
signal_logic: If daily earthquake count in a metro region >5 in 48 hours with avg magnitude >3.5
entry_exit: Entry: If daily earthquake count in a metro region >5 in 48 hours with avg magnitude >3.5 Exit: Exit 10 trading days later or if swarm subsides for 5+ days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use USGS earthquake activity API for daily counts and magnitude by region via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic swarms occur 2–4 times per month globally; US-based swarms fire 1–2 times per month.

schema.idea: Earthquake Swarm Construction Material Demand Surge

data.source: USGS earthquake activity API for daily counts and magnitude by region

schema.behavior: Multi-day earthquake swarms (>5 events in 48 hrs, magnitude 3.5+) in urban areas signal impending rebuilding demand and construction/materials activity.

market_impact.rationale: Post-earthquake reconstruction drives cement, steel, and materials demand.

trade_logic.entry: If daily earthquake count in a metro region >5 in 48 hours with avg magnitude >3.5
trade_logic.exit: Exit 10 trading days later or if swarm subsides for 5+ days

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic swarms occur 2–4 times per month globally; US-based swarms fire 1–2 times per month.

title: Port Container Backlog Volatility Freight Rally
thesis: Week-over-week swings in port container throughput >15% signal volatility in global trade flows and logistics demand. Port volatility creates urgency for shippers to use alternative routes/carriers; freight rates rise.
data_sources: Port of LA/Long Beach container volume weekly data via port_container_volume adapter
signal_logic: If weekly port container volume swings >15% above or below 13-week moving average
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly port container volume swings >15% above or below 13-week moving average Exit: Exit after 4 weeks or when volatility normalizes to <8% of MA
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Port of LA/Long Beach container volume weekly data via port_container_volume adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Container volume swings are seasonal and event-driven; fires 2–4 times per quarter.

market_impact.rationale: Port volatility creates urgency for shippers to use alternative routes/carriers; freight rates rise.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly port container volume swings >15% above or below 13-week moving average
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 4 weeks or when volatility normalizes to <8% of MA

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Container volume swings are seasonal and event-driven; fires 2–4 times per quarter.
